channel.title: Shristi Das Biswas
channel.description: Recent content on Shristi Das Biswas

item.title: About
item.description: Machine Learning enthusiast and Deep Learning researcher at the Centre for Brain-Inspired Computing (C-BRIC), Purdue University. I am a second-year PhD student advised by Prof. Kaushik Roy. My current interests include event-based vision and neuromorphic computing.

item.title: Achievements
item.description: IEEE Women in Engineering Best Student/ Research Scholar Paper Award: Presented at ICDCN 2020. Indian Academy of Sciences Summer Research Fellowship Program 2020: Awarded to top few undergraduate students across the country. Indian Youth Delegate to the People’s Republic of China, 2019. Organised by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India, and the Government of People’s Republic of China.

item.title: Publications
item.description: S. Das Biswas, R. Chakraborty, A. Pramanik, “A Brief Survey on Various Prediction Models for Detection of ADHD from Brain MRI Images,” 21st International Conference on Distributed Computing and Networking (ICDCN) 2020. S. Das Biswas, R. Chakraborty, A. Pramanik, “On Prediction Models for the Detection of Autism Spectrum Disorder,” International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Pattern Recognition (CIPR) 2020. S. Das Biswas, R. Chakraborty, A. Pramanik, “Automated Predictive Diagnosis of ADHD Based on Magnetic Resonance Imaging,” Communicated to and under-review: Journal of Neuroscience Methods, Elsevier.
